maori: He aroha whakatō, he aroha puta mai.
english: If you sow love, love will be returned.

maori: Ehara taku toa i te toa takitahi, engari he toa takitini.
english: My strength is not that of the individual, but of the collective.

maori: He aha te mea nui o te ao? He tangata, he tangata, he tangata.
english: What is the greatest thing in the world? It is people, it is people, it is people.

maori: Ka pū te ruha, ka hao te rangatahi.
english: As the old net piles up on shore, the new net goes fishing.

maori: Hutia te rito o te harakeke, kei hea te kōmako e kō?
english: If you remove the heart of the flax bush, where will the bellbird sing?

maori: Ko au te awa, ko te awa ko au.
english: I am the river, and the river is me.

maori: Nāu te rourou, nāku te rourou, ka ora ai te iwi.
english: With your food basket and my food basket, the people will thrive.
